events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: residential job, overcurrent device selection & coordination, observed field condition is breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch. Work is staged (materials ready, work not begun). Need to select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong.

Breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch means the breaker size doesn't match the conductor ampacity. This is a non-compliant condition. Since work is staged, the materials are ready but not installed yet — so the defect is in the staged materials selection. The breaker and conductor must be coordinated per NEC 240. The breaker must protect the conductor per 240.4. Also 110.14(C) termination temp ratings matter — the conductor ampacity must be based on the termination temperature rating.

Decision: fail (non-compliant). Risk: high (mismatched overcurrent protection can lead to conductor overheating/fire). Component conditions: non-compliant (staged materials are wrong selection). Maybe "installed-defective" doesn't apply since not installed yet. So just "non-compliant".

Percent complete: 0 (staged, work not begun).

Value: could be the correct breaker size or conductor size — but we don't have specific numbers. The scenario doesn't give specific amp values. So value = 0.

Confidence: high, say 0.9.

Findings: breaker/conductor ampacity mismatch — overcurrent device not properly coordinated with conductor ampacity.

Actions: Replace either the breaker or the conductor so they are properly coordinated per NEC 240.4; verify 110.14(C) termination temp rating; ensure 75°C ampacity column used for #1 AWG and larger, 60°C for smaller unless equipment rated otherwise.

References: NEC 240.4, 240.6, 110.14(C), 210.8, 210.12.

Let me write the JSON.

],
